_comment: Plugin hook configuration. Loaded automatically when this plugin is enabled. Uses ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} placeholder so hooks resolve correctly regardless of where the plugin is installed.

PreToolUse._comment: GAP-G (v2.5.1): headless EnterPlanMode redirect. Detects `claude -p`/`--print` via parent-process command-line + permission_mode envelope field; denies EnterPlanMode in headless mode with forcing instruction redirecting to TodoWrite. Closes scenario-04 silent-failure where ExitPlanMode hung on the approval dialog and terminated the session before edits executed.

pre-spawn-tool-mask UNREGISTERED per ADR-0012: empirical probes proved a PreToolUse hook's updatedInput is INERT for Agent/Task spawns (the Agent tool_input has no `tools` field; updatedInput tool/prompt rewrites are not honored). Its network-strip never took effect. Capability enforcement is STATIC — the agent.md frontmatter tools: definition (harness-honored) + the agent.md<->contract reconciliation validator (contracts-validate.js). NETWORK (2026-05-31): the axis decomposes into tool-mediated egress (WebFetch/WebSearch/MCP — enforced via agents/<name>.md tools:) + Bash-subprocess egress (audited advisory by observability/network-egress-audit.js, PostToolUse:Bash). Static reconciliation of a `network` axis is NOT viable (no tools: referent); real prevention is ContainerAdapter-tier. NOT a pending follow-up.

H.9.11: ordered FIRST among content-validators (after config-guard security gate); structural-before-semantic ordering. validate-yaml-frontmatter detects duplicate top-level YAML keys at Edit/Write time on HT-state.md; closes drift-note 80 URGENT 5-recurrence escalation.

v2.9.0 FIX-I9: PreToolUse:Bash observability gate. Detects Bash redirects (>, >>, tee) targeting config-guard-protected files. WARN-not-BLOCK by default (architect.theo HIGH-5: avoid false-positive cliff). STRICT_CONFIG_GUARD=1 env var escalates to block. See kb:design-pushback/syntactic-gate-extension-for-tool-bypass for the design rationale.

PostToolUse._comment: network-egress-audit: ADVISORY (class 1) detection of Bash egress to hosts NOT in any persona's network_* trait (the 'audited post-hoc' the registry's network_anthropic._doc promises). The network axis decomposes into tool-mediated egress (WebFetch/WebSearch/MCP — already enforced via agents/<name>.md tools:) + Bash-subprocess egress (curl/wget/nc — this hook). It is audit, NOT prevention: per-spawn injection is inert (ADR-0012) and PreToolUse:Bash pattern-DENY is evadable theater; real prevention is ContainerAdapter-tier. The contracts-validate.js network comment (un-reconciled by design — no tools: referent) is the static-side companion to this runtime audit.

v3.0 Phase 1 prototype (P-Proto). Captures an L_spawn record envelope to ~/.claude/spawn-state/<run_id>/spawn-<id>.json on every Agent|Task spawn close. Empirical anchor for RFC v3.2-LOCKED's parent-records design (§Pivot 1 + §L_spawn record). Phase 1 scope: axioms + bounded sample fingerprint only; delta capture and parent_state_id chain deferred. Fail-soft per ADR-0001.

v3.1 PR-3b spawn-close-resolver: the 3rd PostToolUse:Agent|Task hook (after kb-citation-gate, spawn-record) and the FIRST production importer of post-spawn-resolver.resolve(). OBSERVE, DON'T ALLOCATE (OQ-21 #181, ADR-0012): the harness already CREATES the isolation:worktree and returns tool_response.worktreePath/worktreeBranch/agentId/toolStats here, live + git-diffable; the kernel cannot inject its own worktree (ADR-0012), so this hook only OBSERVES the harness worktree at close — no pre-spawn allocator, so dormancy-assertion-k1 stays green. SHADOW-ONLY: resolve() runs with a dry-run promoteDeltaFn ({outcome:'PROMOTED',dryRun:true}) + a guarded read-only runGitFn (status/diff/rev-parse only; mutating args refused) — the real INV-20 closure + real K14 scope-detection + RESOLVER_TABLE dispatch run, the verdict is JOURNALED to ~/.claude/spawn-state/<run_id>/resolver-journal-<agentId>.jsonl, but NO git mutation occurs. The K13 serial-marker release is ALSO skipped in shadow (no-op readMarkerFn/releaseSerialMarkerFn seams) so the hook does ZERO spawn-state filesystem mutation, not just zero git — observe-only is honored for K13 state too (journaled k13_skipped:true). A worktree is NOT a security sandbox (Wave-1 p-writescope: absolute-path writes escape the contained delta). The enforcing path (real cherry-pick promote + worktree cleanup + LOOM_RESOLVER_ENFORCE flag) is PR-3c. Fail-soft per ADR-0001: every path exits 0; a crash never bricks a spawn.

Stop._comment: GAP-F (v2.5.0): deterministic context-size warning. Inserted LAST in Stop chain so its forcing instruction is the final concatenation when multiple Stop hooks emit. Uses transcript_path bytes as the primary signal (ground-truth); falls back to turn counter if transcript_path missing.
